page: Microsoft Azure E16as_v4 is an EASv4 family virtual machine configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server features 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and includes 262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core testing. Multi-core performance under stress-ng and PassMark CPU evaluations falls within the average range. Memory performance is a notable strength, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, although uncached memory read and write bandwidths remain average. The server exhibits average performance across standard operations, including Redis SET operations, static web serving throughput, and single-threaded compression and decompression.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core positions this instance as a memory-optimized resource. This density provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. However, a key tradeoff is visible in complex computational tasks; LLM inference speeds for medium 7B models are weak, ranking in the bottom 25%. Consequently, the E16as_v4 is best suited for memory-intensive applications such as relational databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing tasks rather than heavy parallel AI workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v4 is an EASv4 family virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-optimized 8.0 GB per core ratio. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers dedicated CPU allocation with 8 physical cores. The instance includes 262 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU and memory scores, while multi-core processing, Redis operations, and web serving throughput remain average. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in LLM inference for medium models, which ranks in the bottom 25%. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial RAM without extreme compute scaling. It is highly suited for database hosting, caching layers, and enterprise application deployments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine with 16 vCPUs, 128.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features strong single-core and memory performance, making it suitable for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16as_v4 is an EASv4 virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128.0 GB RAM, and a 3.525 GHz AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and high memory density for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM providing an 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor operating at 3.525 GHz
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Optimal for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
